description_long: This object consists of two neatly stacked piles of newspapers, each pile containing several individual newspapers with visible headlines and colored images on the front pages. The newspapers are slightly unevenly stacked, giving a realistic, used appearance, and the edges of the papers are slightly curled and irregular. The front pages display recognizable mastheads such as 'Financial Times' and other publications, with a mix of text and images in black, blue, and red ink. The stacks are of similar height and size, and the newspapers are made of thin, matte newsprint paper. There are no visible racks, display windows, or payment mechanisms; the newspapers are simply stacked on top of each other. This setup is typical of newspapers temporarily placed for sale or distribution in an urban environment, such as on a sidewalk or near a street vendor.
description: Two stacks of newspapers with visible headlines and colored images, arranged in a slightly uneven, realistic pile. The newspapers are made of thin, matte paper and show no racks or display windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing both stacks of newspapers, with clear visibility of the front pages and mastheads.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness and layering of the newspaper stacks, with the edges of the papers more prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite front view with a clear look at the 'Financial Times' masthead and colored images on the top newspaper.
description_view_3: Rear side view showing the backs and edges of the stacks, with less detail of the front pages.
